comment: Strict premise-source audit for the compact PaperInterface surface. Implementation-only PostPaperAudit helper premises are excluded from this ledger.

assumption_section3_access_fraction_doma.reason: The Section 3 proof fixes a base-feature cohort and explicitly takes the access share to be an interior fraction. Lean records the nonnegative and below-one parts used by the compact Theorem 3.1 endpoints.

hC_nonneg : \u2200 e base, 0 \u2264 acce.source_location: source.txt lines 1135-1137: the source writes the relevant access fraction as C in (0, 1).
hC_nonneg : \u2200 e base, 0 \u2264 acce.reason: A source fraction in (0, 1) is nonnegative.

hC_lt_one : \u2200 e base, accessFractio.source_location: source.txt lines 1135-1137: the proof assumes the fixed-cohort access fraction C is in (0, 1).
hC_lt_one : \u2200 e base, accessFractio.reason: The Lean premise is the upper-bound part of the paper's interior-fraction condition.

assumption_section3_source_equilibrium_i.reason: The Section 3 theorems are statements about every source equilibrium under the optional-reporting or report-required source game.

hEq : \u2200 e, paperSourceEquilibrium (.source_location: source.txt lines 229-271 define student decisions and equilibrium; lines 424-437 state Theorem 3.1 for every equilibrium under optional reporting.
hEq : \u2200 e, paperSourceEquilibrium (.reason: This is the paper's source equilibrium premise specialized to the optional-reporting source model.

hEq : \u2200 e, paperSourceEquilibrium (.source_location: source.txt lines 229-271 define student decisions and equilibrium; lines 431-437 state the report-required branch.
hEq : \u2200 e, paperSourceEquilibrium (.reason: This is the paper's source equilibrium premise specialized to the report-required source model.

assumption_source_model_event_and_consis.reason: The paper's source model contains reporting/taking events and estimation-consistency components of equilibrium; Lean names these predicates explicitly when instantiating the source games.

reporterEvent : Equilibrium \u2192 Base.source_location: source.txt lines 242-263 define reporting decisions and equilibrium over students with access; lines 424-437 state the reporting branch.
reporterEvent : Equilibrium \u2192 Base.reason: This is the Lean predicate for the source reporting event used to form event shares.

takerEvent : Equilibrium \u2192 Base \u2.source_location: source.txt lines 242-263 define test-taking decisions and equilibrium over students with access; lines 431-437 state the taking/report-required branch.
takerEvent : Equilibrium \u2192 Base \u2.reason: This is the Lean predicate for the source test-taking event used to form event shares.

estimationConsistent : Equilibrium \u219.source_location: source.txt lines 256-271, Definition 1 includes estimation consistency in equilibrium.
estimationConsistent : Equilibrium \u219.reason: The Lean premise names the paper's estimation-consistency component of source equilibrium.

reportEstimationConsistent takeEstimatio.source_location: source.txt lines 256-271, Definition 1 includes estimation consistency; lines 492-501 state the observed-access source equilibrium setting.
reportEstimationConsistent takeEstimatio.reason: These are the optional-reporting and report-required estimation-consistency propositions passed to the fully specified observed-access source games.

assumption_section3_threshold_decision_s.reason: The paper's Section 3 proof establishes threshold-shaped reporting/taking decisions and the named theorem states finite thresholds in both regimes.

hthreshold : \u2200 e base actor, report.source_location: source.txt lines 424-430 state the optional-reporting finite test-score threshold; lines 1045-1047 describe rewriting the reporting function by a cutoff.
hthreshold : \u2200 e base actor, report.reason: The Lean predicate is the paper's threshold rule for score reporting.

hthreshold : \u2200 e base actor, takeDe.source_location: source.txt lines 431-437 state the report-required finite skill threshold.
hthreshold : \u2200 e base actor, takeDe.reason: The Lean predicate is the paper's threshold rule for test-taking/reporting in the report-required branch.

assumption_section3_zero_positive_event_.reason: The fairness-impossibility proof splits off the zero-reporter/taker branch as the already-test-blank/no-test-relevance case.

hblank_of_no_positive : \u2200 e base, n.source_location: source.txt lines 455-463: reporting unravels until no student reports, which leads to the policy being test-blank; lines 1578-1614 give the proof split.
hblank_of_no_positive : \u2200 e base, n.reason: The Lean premise records the paper's zero-positive-event branch of the unraveling argument. It is not an independent proof certificate.

assumption_positive_gaussian_domain_cond.reason: Gaussian scales, variances, and posterior-slope parameters are positive domain facts in the source Gaussian model and its Bayesian posterior formulas.

htestScale : 0 < testScale.source_location: source.txt lines 205-220 define Gaussian feature signals; source variance/scale parameters are positive by model domain.
htestScale : 0 < testScale.reason: The Lean premise is the positive test-signal scale needed by the observed-access Gaussian source game.

hslope : \u2200 e base, 0 < slope e base.source_location: source.txt lines 2318-2394 derive the Bayesian posterior estimate with a positive test-signal contribution.
hslope : \u2200 e base, 0 < slope e base.reason: The slope is the positive coefficient on the observed signal in the Gaussian posterior mean.

hslope : \u2200 base, 0 < slope base.source_location: source.txt lines 2318-2394 derive the Bayesian posterior estimate with a positive test-signal contribution.
hslope : \u2200 base, 0 < slope base.reason: This is the base-indexed version of the positive Gaussian posterior slope.

hslope : \u2200 base : Base, 0 < slope b.source_location: source.txt lines 2318-2394 derive the Bayesian posterior estimate with a positive test-signal contribution.
hslope : \u2200 base : Base, 0 < slope b.reason: This is the typed base-indexed version of the positive Gaussian posterior slope.

hlawTestScale : \u2200 base, 0 < lawTest.source_location: source.txt lines 205-220 define Gaussian feature signals; lines 2318-2394 use the test signal's nonzero positive variance.
hlawTestScale : \u2200 base, 0 < lawTest.reason: A Gaussian test-signal law in the source model has positive scale.

hlawTestScale : \u2200 base : Base, 0 <.source_location: source.txt lines 205-220 define Gaussian feature signals; lines 2318-2394 use the test signal's nonzero positive variance.
hlawTestScale : \u2200 base : Base, 0 <.reason: A Gaussian test-signal law in the source model has positive scale; this is the typed base-indexed theorem signature form.

hextraNoiseVar : 0 < extraNoiseVar.source_location: source.txt lines 2417-2481: Proposition 4.3 compares the Bayesian estimate distributions with additional test information; variances in the Gaussian source model are positive.
hextraNoiseVar : 0 < extraNoiseVar.reason: The extra-signal variance is a Gaussian-domain parameter, not a proof-route certificate.

assumption_observed_access_source_equili.reason: Lemma 4.1 and downstream observed-access results are stated for source equilibria in the optional-reporting and report-required observed-access games.

hReportEq : paperSourceEquilibrium (lg21.source_location: source.txt lines 492-501 state the observed-access Bayesian policy and its equilibrium conclusion; lines 1784-1810 begin the source proof.
hReportEq : paperSourceEquilibrium (lg21.reason: The Lean premise is the optional-reporting source equilibrium instance to which Lemma 4.1 applies.

hTakeEq : paperSourceEquilibrium (lg21Fu.source_location: source.txt lines 492-501 state the observed-access Bayesian policy and its equilibrium conclusion; lines 1784-1810 include the report-required branch setup.
hTakeEq : paperSourceEquilibrium (lg21Fu.reason: The Lean premise is the report-required source equilibrium instance to which Lemma 4.1 applies.
